hawkish_phrases: inflation had moved up since earlier in the year and remained somewhat elevated
progress toward the Committee's 2 percent inflation objective had stalled in 2025
risks to inflation remained tilted to the upside
a more prolonged period of above-target inflation could risk an increase in longer-run expectations
lowering the policy rate further in the context of elevated inflation readings could be misinterpreted as implying diminished policymaker commitment to the 2 percent inflation objective
dovish_phrases: downside risks to employment had risen in recent months
job gains had slowed this year and the unemployment rate had edged up
upside risks to inflation had diminished since earlier in 2025
lowering the target range for the federal funds rate at this meeting was in line with a forward-looking approach
a move toward a more neutral policy stance would help forestall the possibility of a major deterioration in labor market conditions
summary: The FOMC cut rates by 25bp to 3.5-3.75% with a split vote (9-3: two dissenters preferring hold, one preferring 50bp cut), citing elevated downside employment risks despite still-elevated inflation, with forward guidance emphasizing careful assessment of further adjustments.
